meaning: adj.有节制的,适度的,戒酒的,(气候)温和的
examples: The Nile Valley keeps a temperate climate throughout the year.
Although these vegetables adapt well to our temperate climate, they tend to crop poorly.
Much human cultural development took place in temperate regions.
The impression has been created that the Earth has always been a temperate world.
Mr Kwasniewski has been a temperate, even-handed president, rising skilfully above the squabbling of party politics.
